questions.questionText: The cubic unit cell structure of a compound containing cation M and anion X is shown below. When compared to the anion, the cation has smaller ionic radius. Choose the correct statement(s).
options: (A) The empirical formula of the compound is MX.
(B) The cation M and anion X have different coordination geometries.
(C) The ratio of M$$-$$X bond length of the cubic unit cell edge length is 0.866.
(D) The ratio of the ionic radii of cation M to anion X is 0.414.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: If the unit cell of a mineral has cubic close packed (ccp) array of oxygen atoms with m fraction of octahedral holes occupied by aluminium ions and n fraction of tetrahedral holes occupied by magnesium ions, m and n, respectively, are
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
An element X has a body centred cubic (bcc) structure with a cell edge of 200 pm. The density of the element is 5 g cm$$-$$3. The number of atoms present in 300 g of the element X is _______________.

Given : Avogadro constant, NA = 6.0 $$\times$$ 1023 mol$$-$$1.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Atoms of element X form hcp lattice and those of element Y occupy $${2 \over 3}$$ of its tetrahedral voids. The percentage of element X in the lattice is ____________. (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Sodium metal crystallizes in a body centred cubic lattice with unit cell edge length of $$4~\mathop A\limits^o $$. The radius of sodium atom is __________ $$\times ~10^{-1}$$ $$\mathop A\limits^o $$ (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A metal M crystallizes into two lattices :- face centred cubic (fcc) and body centred cubic (bcc) with unit cell edge length of $$2.0$$ and $$2.5\,\mathop A\limits^o $$ respectively. The ratio of densities of lattices fcc to bcc for the metal M is ____________. (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Atom $\mathrm{X}$ occupies the fcc lattice sites as well as alternate tetrahedral voids of the same lattice. The packing efficiency (in %) of the resultant solid is closest to
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The empirical formula for a compound with a cubic close packed arrangement of anions and with cations occupying all the octahedral sites in A<sub>x</sub>B. The value of x is _____________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following represents the lattice structure of $$\mathrm{A_{0.95}O}$$ containing $$\mathrm{A^{2+},A^{3+}}$$ and $$\mathrm{O^{2-}}$$ ions?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In a solid AB, A atoms are in ccp arrangement and B atoms occupy all the octahedral sites. If two atoms from the opposite faces are removed, then the resultant stoichiometry of the compound is AxBy. The value of x is ____________. [nearest integer]
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A metal M forms hexagonal close-packed structure. The total number of voids in 0.02 mol of it is _________ $$\times$$ $$10^{21}$$ (Nearest integer).

(Given $$\mathrm{N_A=6.02\times10^{23}}$$)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the given close packed structure of a salt made of cation X and anion Y shown below (ions of only one face are shown for clarity), the packing fraction is approximately (packing fraction = $${{packing\,efficiency} \over {100}}$$)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements.

Statement I : Frenkel defects are vacancy as well as interstitial defects.

Statement II : Frenkel defect leads to colour in ionic solids due to presence of F-centres.

Choose the most appropriate answer for the statements from the options given below :
options: (A) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An element M crystallises in a body centred cubic unit cell with a cell edge of $$300 \,\mathrm{pm}$$. The density of the element is $$6.0 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~cm}^{-3}$$. The number of atoms present in $$180 \mathrm{~g}$$ of the element is ____________ $$\times 10^{23}$$. (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A cubic solid is made up of two elements X and Y. Atoms of X are present on every alternate corner and one at the center of cube. Y is at $$\frac{1}{3}^{\mathrm{rd}}$$ of the total faces. The empirical formula of the compound is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The arrangement of X$$-$$ ions around A+ ion in solid AX is given in the figure (not drawn to scale). If the radius of X$$-$$ is 250 pm, the radius of A+ is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
An element crystallises in a face-centred cubic (fcc) unit cell with cell edge $$a$$. The distance
between the centres of two nearest octahedral voids in the crystal lattice is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The density (in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{cm}^{-3}$ ) of the metal which forms a cubic close packed (ccp) lattice with an axial distance (edge length) equal to 400 pm is ___________.

Use: Atomic mass of metal $=105.6 \mathrm{amu}$ and Avogadro's constant $=6 \times 10^{23} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The incorrect statement about the imperfections in solids is :
options: (A) Schottky defect decreases the density of the substance.
(B) Interstitial defect increases the density of the substance.
(C) Frenkel defect does not alter the density of the substance.
(D) Vacancy defect increases the density of the substance.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A crystalline solid of a pure substance has a face-centered cubic structure with a cell edge of $$400$$ $$pm.$$ If the density of the substance in the crystal is $$8$$ $$g\,c{m^{ - 3}},$$ then the number of atoms present in $$256$$ $$g$$ of the crystal is $$N \times {10^{24}}.$$ The value of $$N$$ is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Ionic radii of cation $$\mathrm{A}^{+}$$ and anion $$\mathrm{B}^{-}$$ are 102 and $$181 \,\mathrm{pm}$$ respectively. These ions are allowed to crystallize into an ionic solid. This crystal has cubic close packing for $$\mathrm{B}^{-}$$. $$\mathrm{A}^{+}$$ is present in all octahedral voids. The edge length of the unit cell of the crystal AB is __________ pm. (Nearest Integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Iron oxide FeO, crystallises in a cubic lattice with a unit cell edge length of 5.0 Å. If density of the $\mathrm{FeO}$ in the crystal is $4.0 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~cm}^{-3}$, then the number of $\mathrm{FeO}$ units present per unit cell is __________. (Nearest integer)

Given: Molar mass of $\mathrm{Fe}$ and $\mathrm{O}$ is 56 and $16 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ respectively. $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}=6.0 \times 10^{23} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Metal deficiency defect is shown by Fe0.93O. In the crystal, some Fe2+ cations are missing and loss of positive charge is compensated by the presence of Fe3+ ions. The percentage of Fe2+ ions in the Fe0.93O crystals is __________. (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The correct relationships between unit cell edge length '$$a$$' and radius of sphere '$$r$$' for face-centred and body-centred cubic structures respectively are :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In a metal deficient oxide sample, $\mathbf{M}_{\mathbf{x}} \mathbf{Y}_2 \mathbf{O}_4$ ( $\mathbf{M}$ and $\mathbf{Y}$ are metals), $\mathbf{M}$ is present in both +2 and +3 oxidation states and $\mathbf{Y}$ is in +3 oxidation state. If the fraction of $\mathbf{M}^{2+}$ ions present in $\mathbf{M}$ is $\frac{1}{3}$, the value of $\mathbf{X}$ is _______ .
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Metal $$\mathrm{M}$$ crystallizes into a fcc lattice with the edge length of $$4.0 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{~cm}$$. The atomic mass of the metal is ____________ $$\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{mol}$$. (Nearest integer)

$$\left(\right.$$ Use : $$\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}=6.02 \times 10^{23} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$, density of metal, $$\mathrm{M}=9.03 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~cm}^{-3}$$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The distance between Na+ and Cl$$-$$ ions in solid NaCl of density 43.1 g cm$$-$$3 is _______________ $$\times$$ 10$$-$$10 m. (Nearest Integer)

(Given : NA = 6.02 $$\times$$ 1023 mol$$-$$1)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Consider an ionic solid $$MX$$ with $$NaCl$$ structure. Construct a new structure $$(Z)$$ whose unit cell is constructed from the unit cell of $$MX$$ following the sequential instructions given below. Neglect the charge balance.
$$(i)\,\,\,\,\,$$ Remove all the anions $$(X)$$ except the central one

$$(ii)\,\,\,\,$$ Replace all the face centered cations $$(M)$$ by anions $$(X)$$

$$(iii)\,\,$$ Remove all the corner cations $$(M)$$

$$(iv)\,\,\,\,$$ Replace the central anion $$(X)$$ with cation $$(M)$$

The value of $$\,\,\left( {{{number\,\,of\,\,anions} \over {number\,\,of\,\,cations}}} \right)\,\,$$ in $$Z$$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The CORRECT statement(s) for cubic close packed (ccp) three dimensional structure is (are) :
options: (A) The number of the nearest neighbours of an atom present in the topmost layer is 12
(B) The efficiency of an atom packing is 74%
(C) The number of octahedral and tetrahedral voids per atom are 1 and 2, respectively
(D) The unit cell edge length is $$2 \sqrt 2$$ times the radius of the atom
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A compound is formed by two elements $$\mathrm{X}$$ and $$\mathrm{Y}$$. The element $$\mathrm{Y}$$ forms cubic close packed arrangement and those of element $$\mathrm{X}$$ occupy one third of the tetrahedral voids. What is the formula of the compound?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A crystal is made up of metal ions 'M<sub>1</sub>' and 'M<sub>2</sub>'
and oxide ions. Oxide ions form a ccp lattice
structure. The cation 'M<sub>1</sub>' occupies 50% of
octahedral voids and the cation 'M<sub>2</sub>' occupies
12.5% of tetrahedral voids of oxide lattice. The
oxidation numbers of 'M<sub>1</sub>' and 'M<sub>2</sub>' are,
respectively :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following expressions is correct in case of a $\mathrm{CsCl}$ unit cell (edge length 'a')?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
An atomic substance A of molar mass $$12 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$ has a cubic crystal structure with edge length of $$300 ~\mathrm{pm}$$. The no. of atoms present in one unit cell of $$\mathrm{A}$$ is ____________. (Nearest integer)

Given the density of $$\mathrm{A}$$ is $$3.0 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mL}^{-1}$$ and $$\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}=6.02 \times 10^{23} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Number of crystal systems from the following where body centred unit cell can be found, is ____________.

Cubic, tetragonal, orthorhombic, hexagonal, rhombohedral, monoclinic, triclinic
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Atoms of metals $\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}$, and $\mathrm{z}$ form face-centred cubic (fcc) unit cell of edge length $\mathrm{L}_{\mathrm{x}}$, body-centred cubic (bcc) unit cell of edge length $\mathrm{L}_y$, and simple cubic unit cell of edge length $\mathrm{L}_z$, respectively.

If $\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{z}}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} r_{\mathrm{y}} ; \mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{y}}=\frac{8}{\sqrt{3}} \mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{x}} ; M_{\mathrm{z}}=\frac{3}{2} M_{\mathrm{y}}$ and $M_{\mathrm{z}}=3 M_{\mathrm{x}}$, then the correct statement(s) is(are) :

[Given: $M_x, M_y$, and $M_z$ are molar masses of metals $x, y$, and $z$, respectively.
$\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{x}}, \mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{y}}$, and $\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{z}}$ are atomic radii of metals $\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}$, and $\mathrm{z}$, respectively.]
options: (A) Packing efficiency of unit cell of $x>$ Packing efficiency of unit cell of $y>$ Packing efficiency of unit cell of $z$
questions.subject: Chemistry
